subject: Statistics
title: Why Most Bettors Lose
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Why Most Bettors Lose.

hook.text: Sports bettors remember their wins vividly and forget their losses quietly. That's how casinos stay in business.

buildup.text: The vig means you need to win about 52.4% of spread bets just to break even. Most people win about 50%.

discovery.text: Over 1,000 bets, a 50% bettor paying standard vig loses about 4.5% of total wagered. The math is relentless.

twist.text: Cognitive biases compound the problem. Bettors chase losses, overvalue favorites, and bet too much on parlays.

climax.text: The few consistent winners exploit tiny edges with discipline, bankroll management, and statistical models.

punchline.text: The vig plus human bias equals guaranteed long-term loss for most.

quiz.question: Why do most sports bettors lose money over time?
options: The vig requires winning about 52.4% to break even, and cognitive biases make it worse
